disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known genetic syndrome with an inverted sleep-wake pattern; the patient has been awake for more than 24 hours before this crisis
trigger_question_examples: What has his sleep been like?
What was happening before the meltdown started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He has a genetic condition that badly disrupts his sleep, and he had been awake for over a whole day when this started.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Active, violent, repetitive head-banging against hard walls that the carers cannot contain
trigger_question_examples: What is he doing to himself right now?
Can you keep him safe where he is?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He keeps smashing his head against the walls as hard as he can and we cannot stop him or hold him back.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient has forced a small hard foreign body (plastic bead) deep into the right ear canal
trigger_question_examples: You mentioned his ear — what happened there?
Is there anything stuck in his ear?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: In the middle of it he shoved a plastic bead right down into his ear and it is deep now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reduced pain sensitivity — the patient does not guard, withdraw, or stop despite self-inflicted injury
trigger_question_examples: Does he show pain or pull away when he hurts himself?
Does he seem to feel the injuries?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: That's the scary part — he doesn't seem to feel it at all, so he just keeps banging his head without stopping.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Night-time melatonin was taken but was ineffective; no other sedating medication is available at home
trigger_question_examples: Has he had any medication tonight?
Do you have anything at home that calms him?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He had his usual melatonin but it did nothing tonight, and we don't have anything else here to settle him.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No ear bleeding or discharge and no fever currently observed (pertinent negatives)
trigger_question_examples: Is there any blood or fluid coming from his ear?
Does he feel hot or have a temperature?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, there's no blood or liquid from his ear that we can see, and he doesn't feel hot.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient is non-verbal during the crisis and cannot self-report; the parents are the sole informants
trigger_question_examples: Can he tell you what is wrong?
Is he able to talk to us right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He can't tell us anything when he's like this — he's just screaming, so we have to speak for him.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Forehead bruising from head-banging and a hoarse voice from prolonged screaming
trigger_question_examples: Can you see any injuries on him?
How does he look and sound?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His forehead is bruised from hitting the wall and his voice has gone hoarse from all the screaming.
